name: Obwalden (Switzerland)
description: A square flag divided red over white, charged with an upright key counterchanged against the field.
history: The key derives from the 13th-century seal of Unterwalden. The single-key design was adopted for Obwalden in 1816, which also distinguished its flag from the formerly identical red-and-white flag of Solothurn.

features.role: upright key counterchanged (white on the red half, red on the white half)
features.symbolism: the Keys of Heaven of Unterwalden
